description: Kinematic demonstration episodes for six robot arm gestures (greet, nod, point, idle, listen, farewell) recorded in MuJoCo simulation and/or manually authored as joint-angle keyframe sequences. Each episode is a sequence of (joint_angles, gripper) observations paired with target joint-angle action chunks used to train the ACT imitation-learning policy.

fields.observation.state: float32[6] - joint angles in radians at each timestep
fields.action: float32[6] - target joint angles for the next chunk_size steps

pii.notes: Robot kinematics only - no human motion capture data

known_limitations: Phase 1 episodes are simulation-only; real-hardware episodes pending Phase 2 deployment
Keyframe interpolation produces smooth but not anthropomorphic motion
ACT policy trained on short episodes may not generalise to novel start poses
bias_notes: All gestures authored in a forward-facing, table-height context - may not transfer to different mounting angles
Western gesture conventions assumed; cross-cultural gesture appropriateness not evaluated
